# Autonomous Email Triage (Turdus)

A no-human-in-the-loop email sweep designed to run unattended on an hourly cadence inside the **Turdus** daemon (T3, aauth_sub turdus@ateles-swarm). It is the autonomous counterpart to the interactive `email-triage` skill. They share storage and classification conventions but differ in control flow: this skill NEVER pauses for confirmation and NEVER takes an irreversible outward action.

## Hard boundaries (non-negotiable)
- NEVER send an email. Only ever create Gmail *drafts* (create_draft). A draft is inert until the operator opens Gmail and sends it. No auto-send path, ever, regardless of how routine the reply looks.
- NEVER archive or modify labels beyond Turdus's own processed-label bookkeeping. Archiving stays a human action (interactive email-triage skill or operator).
- NEVER delete anything.
- These boundaries hold even if a caller passes flags asking to relax them. Outbound sending/archiving would be a separate, explicitly-approved skill — not this one.

## Phase 0 — Kill switch (check FIRST, every run)
Before any drafting or notifying, check the operator's pause control. Two layers, either of which pauses the autonomous behavior:
- Env var TURDUS_AUTODRAFT_ENABLED — if unset or not '1'/'true', drafting + notifying are paused.
- Neotoma flag (a singleton config / execution_policy entity Turdus retrieves, e.g. key turdus_autodraft_paused) — lets the operator pause/resume WITHOUT redeploying or touching the host: flip it in the Neotoma inspector and the next sweep honors it.
When paused: still run Phase 1 storage (memorization is read-only and never stops), but skip Phase 3 drafting and Phase 4 notifications entirely, and emit a single low-priority daemon_report noting the run was paused (and which layer caused it). Default posture on first deploy is PAUSED — the operator explicitly enables drafting once they've watched a dry run.

## Phase 1 — Thorough 24h sweep (store regardless of inbox)
Memorization completeness: every email from the last 24h must end up in Neotoma whether still in inbox, archived, read, or filtered past the inbox.
1. `gws gmail users messages list --params '{\"userId\":\"me\",\"q\":\"newer_than:1d\",\"maxResults\":500}'` — q:newer_than:1d spans all labels (INBOX, archived, SENT), capturing mail the inbox-only poll misses. Paginate on nextPageToken.
2. For each message ID, check Neotoma for an existing email/email_message entity by message_id (retrieve_entity_by_identifier). Skip ones already stored — dedup by gmail:<id> so re-runs are idempotent and cheap.
3. For each NEW message store an email entity (declared fields message_id, subject, from_address, from_name, to, received_at, summary, thread_id; data_source in raw_fragments). Storage floor: no purely 'no action' outcome — every thread is stored.
4. Extract derived entities as the interactive skill does: contacts, transactions, events, organizations; for invoices with payment due a task assigned to Monedula (urgent) per Turdus's standing invoice-routing rule.
Keep per-run cost bounded — dedup means a steady hourly run only works on the few genuinely new messages.

## Phase 2 — Classify (same taxonomy as email-triage)
Actionable / Awaiting reply / Informational / No-action, same heuristics incl. the GitHub-CI rule (run-failures → deduped (repository, component) tasks; PR/issue comments → no task; noreply@github.com security notices → leave in inbox). Invoices with payment due are Actionable.

## Phase 3 — Prepare drafts for actionable mail (no send)
For each Actionable message, FIRST fetch the full thread and inspect every message's labelIds to determine reply state. CRITICAL — a DRAFT is NOT a SENT reply:
- Only a message bearing the SENT label (and newer than the inbound) counts as 'already replied'. internalDate of that SENT message must be >= the actionable inbound's internalDate.
- A message bearing the DRAFT label is an unsent draft — it does NOT satisfy the reply gate. Never treat DRAFT as SENT. (Common failure: a thread-summary that buckets every non-SENT message as 'received' or that reads a DRAFT's date as a reply — always read labelIds explicitly, do not infer from date/position.)

Branch on reply state:
(a) Newer SENT reply exists → already replied. Skip drafting; classify as Awaiting reply or no-action. Do not surface in the action digest.
(b) An unsent DRAFT already exists in the thread (DRAFT label, no newer SENT) → DO NOT create a second competing draft (that would risk clobbering or duplicating an in-progress human/agent draft). Instead: store/refresh a draft_message entity pointing at the EXISTING Gmail draft ID (status pending_approval, notes flag 'pre-existing draft, surfaced for review/send'), and surface it in the action digest as 'draft ready (existing) — review & send'. Leave the Gmail draft untouched.
(c) No SENT and no DRAFT → draft normally:
  1. Use existing context before drafting — pull the thread's Neotoma records (sender contact + notes, prior emails, linked plan/payment_profile, operator's prior drafts) and check Google Calendar (gws calendar events list, Europe/Madrid) for scheduling threads. Turdus drafts from memory, not from the email alone.
  2. Create a Gmail draft with create_draft, passing replyToMessageId so it threads as a proper reply. Match language/register; follow interactive drafting rules (concise, no filler, Spanish sign-offs, single-post social rules).
  3. Also store the draft as a draft_message entity (status pending_approval; to/subject/body/recipient_contact_id/notes incl. in-reply-to message ID AND the returned Gmail draft ID) so the operator can open it in Gmail or the Neotoma inspector.
  4. Create/refresh the actionable task (audience=agent → Apis, or assigned_to monedula for payments), linking the draft_message and source email.
If an Actionable message needs a human decision (e.g. approve a fee), do NOT fabricate a reply — create the task, flag it in the action digest as decision-needed, skip the draft.

## Phase 4 — Notify the operator (two Telegram digests)
Use Turdus's existing Notifier (Telegram, Turdus topic, priority_rubric). At most two messages per run; send NOTHING when a run produced no new actionable or noteworthy informational mail (silence is correct for an idle hour).
A. Action digest (priority per rubric; only if >=1 actionable this run): one line per actionable thread (sender, gist, what's needed); for fresh drafts '✏️ draft ready' + Neotoma inspector link to the draft_message (also in Gmail Drafts); for pre-existing drafts '✏️ draft ready (existing) — review & send' + link; decision-needed items stated in one line, no draft; invoices/payments grouped, noted as routed to Monedula.
B. Informational digest (low priority; only if >=1 worth knowing): short summary per informational thread so the operator gets substance without opening Gmail. Exclude pure noise (CI, OTP, marketing) — stored and left for archiving, not surfaced.
Hygiene: respect priority_rubric; never exceed two messages/run; batch not per-email; if Telegram fails, log and continue (storage already happened).

## Phase 5 — Self-audit before exit
Verify every new in-window message has an email entity (no silent drops); every actionable thread has a draft+task, a surfaced pre-existing draft, or a decision-flagged task; unknown_fields_count is 0; idempotency held; reply gate correctly distinguished SENT from DRAFT. Log a one-line run summary (stored/actionable/drafted/existing-drafts-surfaced/decisions/informational + paused-mode flag) as a daemon_report.

## Relationship to other skills/daemons
email-triage (interactive) = human-driven version with sending, archiving, and the per-thread review table; this skill never sends or archives. Turdus daemon owns the schedule, Gmail creds, Notifier, and the kill switch. Monedula receives payment tasks (unchanged). Apis receives agent-audience tasks (unchanged).

## Do not
- Send any email — drafts only, always.
- Archive, delete, or relabel beyond processed-bookkeeping.
- Notify on an empty run — silence when nothing new.
- Exceed two Telegram messages per run.
- Skip storage for any in-window message — memorization completeness is the point.
- Fabricate a reply for a decision that's the operator's — flag it, don't draft it.
- Re-store already-stored mail — dedup by message_id so hourly runs stay cheap and idempotent.
- Draft or notify while the kill switch is paused — check it first every run; when paused, store only.
- Treat a DRAFT in the thread as a SENT reply — read labelIds explicitly; a draft never satisfies the reply gate, and an existing draft must be surfaced, not duplicated.
